headline: Meta (META) Faces AI Support Breach As Key Internal AI Executive Exits

In a separate development, a senior executive overseeing Meta's internal AI initiatives is leaving the company during an extensive AI transition. headline: Manus original investors plan to buy back AI firm from Meta for $2 billion, The Information reports
summary: The early Chinese backers of AI startup Manus are planning to buy the company back from Meta at ‌the $2 billion price that the Facebook parent paid, The Information ‌reported on Thursday, citing two people with direct knowledge of the matter.  The reported move comes ​months after China ordered Meta to unwind its acquisition of Manus, amid Beijing's tightening scrutiny of U.S. investment in Chinese startups developing advanced AI technologies.  Early investors in Manus, including HSG, ZhenFund and Tencent are participating in the buyback, ‌The Information said, adding that ⁠HSG and ZhenFund are considering using fresh capital to acquire Meta's position in the startup.

content.title: Manus original investors plan to buy back AI firm from Meta for $2 billion, The Information reports
content.summary: The early Chinese backers of AI startup Manus are planning to buy the company back from Meta at ‌the $2 billion price that the Facebook parent paid, The Information ‌reported on Thursday, citing two people with direct knowledge of the matter.  The reported move comes ​months after China ordered Meta to unwind its acquisition of Manus, amid Beijing's tightening scrutiny of U.S. investment in Chinese startups developing advanced AI technologies.  Early investors in Manus, including HSG, ZhenFund and Tencent are participating in the buyback, ‌The Information said, adding that ⁠HSG and ZhenFund are considering using fresh capital to acquire Meta's position in the startup.

headline: Israeli cyber startup Dream raises $260 million, valued at $3 billion
summary: Israeli AI cybersecurity startup Dream, co-founded by the former head of spyware firm NSO Group, said it ‌raised $260 million in a private funding round, bringing its valuation to $3 billion.  Co-founder ‌Shalev Hulio, who cut ties with NSO in 2022 after the company was accused by Meta of ​targeting WhatsApp and its users, went on to launch Dream in 2023.  Hulio co-founded NSO in 2010 and previously served as its CEO.

content.title: Israeli cyber startup Dream raises $260 million, valued at $3 billion
content.summary: Israeli AI cybersecurity startup Dream, co-founded by the former head of spyware firm NSO Group, said it ‌raised $260 million in a private funding round, bringing its valuation to $3 billion.  Co-founder ‌Shalev Hulio, who cut ties with NSO in 2022 after the company was accused by Meta of ​targeting WhatsApp and its users, went on to launch Dream in 2023.  Hulio co-founded NSO in 2010 and previously served as its CEO.

headline: One of Google’s AI Leaders Is Joining OpenAI. The Talent War Continues.
summary: One of the world’s leading artificial-intelligence researchers is leaving  Alphabet  for OpenAI, dealing a blow to the Google parent in the war for AI talent.  Noam Shazeer, who co-led the development of Google’s Gemini AI models, announced his departure in a social media post Wednesday night.  The move comes less than two years after Google doled out $2.7 billion to bring back Shazeer after he left the company to found a start-up in 2021.
